inline_comments.message:
One issue if copied from api. etc .. docker set up is that the major.minor-SNAPSHOT-latest tag is not pushed.   That's a shortcoming in those components.   Is it possible to support it?

Take it with a grain of salt, but this is a reference on the tags that were applied before.

	BUILD:
		verify:
			onap/policy-pdpd-cl:latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4-SNAPSHOT-latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4.0-SNAPSHOT-20190606T212206Z
		merge:
			onap/policy-pdpd-cl:latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4-SNAPSHOT-latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4.0-SNAPSHOT-20190606T212206Z
		release:
			onap/policy-pdpd-cl:latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4-SNAPSHOT-latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4.0-SNAPSHOT-STAGING-20190606T212206Z
	PUSH:
		verify: 
		merge:
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4-SNAPSHOT-latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4.0-SNAPSHOT-20190606T212206Z
		release:
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4-SNAPSHOT-latest
			nexus3.onap.org:10003/onap/policy-pdpd-cl:1.4.0-SNAPSHOT-STAGING-20190606T212206Z
there's a newer version I think ..
general - do you want to add your company as a modified copyright?
I just observed it is not tagging SNAPSHOT-latest correctly, although the groovy script does generate the right tag. Sure I will take a look at this
Yes 0.30. I will bump it, just took the same version as api and pap. I think this version must be maintained in the parent pom

by default the docker-maven-plugin outputs the artifacts defined by dependencySet config into target/<image-name in plugin config>/build/maven root. I could specify an outputDirectory but it would create a subpath in maven dir

I am struggling with this "/maven/".  I think the path is relative to the underlying "docker build" working dir.  Is it correct?  If that's the case, will be more intuitive to use maven/..  Even better to not have it maven plugin specific (migrating to other may change this) would be to keep it as it was, generic in the source "." directory as it will be generic and not maven plugins specific. It will imply to put more intelligence in the poms to place it there.  If you could consider.
All this JIRA is about is moving to using docker plugin exactly like the api, pap repos. How is what is happening here different from those repos? Looks the same to me.

will COPY be able to find the .sh scripts?

also, why bother to COPY the zip, only to remove it later?
